name: Chicken Breast
summary: A lean, high-protein staple that's also a good source of selenium and the B-vitamins niacin and B6.

benefits.mechanism: Chicken breast is one of the leanest sources of complete protein — supplying all essential amino acids the body needs to build and repair muscle — with little saturated fat once the skin is removed.

citations.title: Protein — The Nutrition Source, Harvard T.H. Chan School of Public Health

benefits.claim: Good source of selenium and B-vitamins
benefits.mechanism: Chicken supplies selenium — a mineral built into antioxidant enzymes and needed for thyroid hormone — along with niacin and vitamin B6, B-vitamins that power energy metabolism.

citations.title: Selenium — NIH Office of Dietary Supplements (Health Professional Fact Sheet)

surprisingFacts.text: Cooked chicken breast is roughly 31% protein by weight — one of the highest protein-to-calorie ratios of any whole food, which is why it's a staple of physique diets.

cautions.effect: Nothing specific — it's lean protein. Very high protein intakes matter only with existing kidney disease.
